Entertainment.detail: Stephen Colbert concluded his 11-season run of 'The Late Show' on CBS with a finale featuring celebrity cameos and musical performances, marking the end of an era for the late-night franchise. The show said goodbye after 33 years on air.
Entertainment.headline: Stephen Colbert's Late Show finale was a bittersweet, star-packed goodbye
Ex-girlfriend of boy band member charged with trying to hire a hitman to kill him
Entertainment.detail: The ex-girlfriend of a boy band member has been charged with attempting to hire a hitman. The details surrounding the case are still emerging.
Nicole Borromeo is inviting Filipinos to support her journey in the Miss Worldwide competition. She encourages them to be a part of her experience.
Entertainment.headline: Nicole Borromeo invites Filipinos to join her on Miss Worldwide 'adventure'

Entertainment.detail: Kyle Busch, a NASCAR star, has passed away at the age of 41 due to a severe illness. His family announced his hospitalization earlier on Thursday.
Entertainment.headline: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dies at 41 after a severe illness, his family says
'We've got 25 to 30% already shot': sequel to Michael Jackson biopic on way, says studio
Entertainment.detail: The studio has announced that 25-30% of the sequel to the Michael Jackson biopic has already been filmed. This suggests that the project is well underway.

Zimbabwe.headline: Wave of vandalism hits Harare's prepaid water meters
Zimbabwe.detail: Reports indicate a surge in vandalism targeting prepaid water meters in Harare, disrupting water services for residents.
Zimbabwe is experiencing a surge in commercial milk production, signaling positive growth in the agricultural sector.
Zimbabwe.headline: Commercial milk production surges
Police crackdown on knife crime as violent attacks surge
Zimbabwe.detail: In response to a rise in violent attacks, police have launched a crackdown on the carrying of knives and machetes in public areas.

Zimbabwe.headline: Judicial Service Commission rolls out IECMS awareness campaign
Zimbabwe.detail: The Judicial Service Commission is conducting an awareness campaign for the Integrated Electronic Case Management System (IECMS).

Zimbabwe.headline: Chivayo withdraws charges against ex-wife and her mother after nights in custody
Zimbabwe.detail: Wicknell Chivayo has withdrawn the charges against his ex-wife, Sonja Madzikanda, and her mother after they spent several nights in custody.

World.detail: The IEA has issued warnings about 'the most severe disruption' ever, with the agency chief stating, "We are facing the biggest energy security threat in history."
World.headline: Strait Of Hormuz Shutdown, Oil Supply Crisis Triggers 'Red Zone' Warning
Farmers Turn to Human Urine After Fertiliser Crunch From Iran War
World.detail: Due to fertilizer shortages resulting from the Iran war, farmers are increasingly using human urine as an alternative.
During a meeting in Beijing, Xi Jinping reportedly told Donald Trump that Vladimir Putin may come to regret the war in Ukraine.
World.headline: Putin May "Regret" Ukraine War: Xi Jinping Told Trump During Beijing Trip

World.detail: Two separate attacks in northern Honduras resulted in the deaths of at least 16 people, one during a police raid and another at a palm farm where rural laborers were killed.
World.headline: At least 16 people killed in two attacks in northern Honduras
Police urge new witnesses to speak out in Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor inquiry
World.detail: Police have requested that new witnesses come forward in the Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor inquiry, marking the first major update since his arrest in February regarding misconduct allegations.

Education.headline: CBSE Students Have 35 Hours Left To Apply For Answer Copies After Glitches
Education.detail: Due to technical issues, the deadline to apply for scanned answer copies has been extended, giving CBSE Class 12 students only 35 hours to submit their applications.

Education.headline: Delhi University To Accept GATE Scores For M.Tech Admissions For First Time
Education.detail: Delhi University will now accept GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) scores for admissions to its M.Tech programs, opening a new avenue for engineering graduates.
The admit card for the All India Bar Examination (AIBE) 21 is expected to be released today, and candidates should check the official website for instructions and a direct link to download it.
Education.headline: AIBE 21 Admit Card 2026 To Be Out Today: Check Hall Ticket Steps, Direct Link Soon
IIIT Bangalore Begins Registration For BTech And Integrated MTech Courses 2026-27
Education.detail: The Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Bangalore has commenced the registration process for its B.Tech and integrated M.Tech programs for the academic year 2026-27.

Education.headline: Harvard faculty vote to limit A grades for undergraduates
Education.detail: In a closely watched vote, Harvard faculty have decided to take strong action to combat grade inflation by limiting the number of students who can receive A grades.

Health.headline: Kissei ordered to add warning over vasculitis drug after fatalities
Health.detail: The health ministry in Japan has ordered Kissei Pharmaceutical to add a warning to the package insert of Tavneos, a drug for vasculitis, after 20 patients died after taking the medicine and developing liver dysfunction. The ministry is also investigating a possible relationship with the deaths, as vasculitis itself is a severe disease.

A workshop in South Africa addressed the growing challenge of post-amputation pain, which affects sleeping patterns, mobility, mental health, and overall quality of life.  Dr. Katleho Limakatso noted the increasing burden of limb amputations globally and the need for greater research and collaboration in care.
Health.headline: Post-amputation pain – a 'growing national health challenge'

Health.detail: North Carolina lawmakers are targeting hospitals, particularly Atrium Health, with reform bills aimed at increasing transparency and controlling executive compensation. Proposed legislation includes giving state officials authority to review hospital transactions, adding whistleblower protections, and capping nonprofit hospital CEO pay.
Health.headline: Lawmakers target hospitals – and Atrium Health in particular – in reform bills

Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) Director-General Tedros Adhanom stated that the Ebola outbreak in Congo is spreading rapidly, and the risk assessment at the national level has been upgraded to "very high". There have been 82 confirmed cases and 7 confirmed deaths, with almost 750 suspected cases and 177 suspected deaths.
Health.headline: WHO chief says Ebola outbreak in Congo is 'spreading rapidly' and upgrades risk assessment
Summaries of health policy coverage from major news organizations.
Health.detail: KFF Health News provides summaries of health policy coverage from major news organizations, including original stories like "3 Medical Routines That Older People May Not Need" and "Trump's $50B Rural Health Bet Meets a Healthcare Desert in North Carolina". These stories address issues such as unnecessary screenings and treatments for older adults and the challenges of improving rural healthcare access.

Lifestyle.detail: Researchers found evidence of slowed aging from lifestyle behaviors like healthy eating and exercise as part of a major clinical trial, demonstrating that accessible interventions that make up a healthy lifestyle can protect cognitive function. The study, based on the Alzheimer's Association's U.S. POINTER trial, suggests programs offering guidance and accountability could be especially effective at keeping seniors healthy as they age.
Lifestyle.headline: Lifestyle Programs May Slow Aging in Seniors: Study

Lifestyle.detail: The Melbourne Vixens have revealed their 2026 First Nations Dress, designed by Tarni Jarvis, honoring the club's journey after its 2025 premiership success and its connection to Country and community. The dress, designed around the theme of leadership transfer, was unveiled during a special presentation that included a smoking ceremony and Welcome to Country.
Lifestyle.headline: Melbourne Vixens unveil 2026 First Nations dress

Lifestyle.detail: The Newcastle Knights have partnered with Australian lifestyle brand SŚAINT to launch a limited-edition merchandise capsule, blending streetwear with the Knights' identity. This collaboration highlights the growing intersection of fashion and sport, with the capsule available via the SŚAINT website and in-stadium.
Lifestyle.headline: Knights and SŚAINT launch groundbreaking collaboration

Lifestyle.detail: The Strada della Forra, carved into the Brasa River gorge, connects Lake Garda to the Tremosine plateau and is considered by many to be the most beautiful road in the world. This dramatic stretch of road attracts motorcyclists, drivers, cyclists, and photographers with its cinematic curves and breathtaking views.
Lifestyle.headline: The most beautiful road in the world is in Italy

Lifestyle.detail: Today in history for May 22nd includes significant events such as the strongest earthquake recorded in 1960 in southern Chile, and President Lyndon B. Johnson outlining the goals of his “Great Society” in 1964. Also highlighted are the birthdays of notable figures like Richard Benjamin, Bernie Taupin, and Morrissey.
Lifestyle.headline: Today in history • Friday, May 22, 2026

Politics.detail: The U.S. and Iran have signaled progress in talks to end the war, but the combatants remain at loggerheads over Tehran's enriched uranium stockpile and tolls on the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday said there were “good signs” that an agreement to end the conflict is in sight.
Politics.headline: U.S., Iran Signal Peace Progress — But Remain At Odds Over Enriched Uranium, Strait Of Hormuz Tolls
Trump, Facing GOP Blowback, Sends 5,000 Troops To Poland
Politics.detail: President Trump said Thursday that the U.S. is sending 5,000 additional troops to Poland, a week after the Pentagon canceled the long-planned deployment of 4,000 service members to the Central European country. Lawmakers have expressed deep frustration and concern over the move to halt the planned rotation, accusing the administration of making a last-minute move with no consultation with Congress.

Politics.headline: US deepens European uncertainty with deployment of 5,000 troops to Poland
Politics.detail: The United States is further solidifying its military presence in Europe by deploying an additional 5,000 troops to Poland. This move comes amidst existing uncertainties and ongoing discussions about European security and defense strategies.

House Republicans have canceled a vote on a war powers resolution aimed at ending the U.S. war in Iran. The decision comes amid ongoing tensions and debates regarding the extent of U.S. military involvement in the region.
Politics.headline: House Republicans cancel vote on war powers resolution to end US war in Iran.

Politics.detail: Industry leaders warned in last-minute calls to the president that the proposed safety vetting system could inhibit development of the pivotal technology.
Politics.headline: Pressure from Silicon Valley helped block Trump's expected order on AI.

Technology.detail: Reportedly, Trump's AI order was delayed due to last-minute pressure from industry leaders who expressed concerns.
Technology.headline: Trump Postpones AI Oversight Executive Order

Technology.detail: SpaceX disclosed in its S-1 filing that its Redmond, Wash., facility produces approximately 70 Starlink satellites per week, putting hard production numbers on the operation for the first time.
Technology.headline: SpaceX is churning out 70 Starlink satellites a week in Redmond, and other tidbits from its IPO filing

Technology.detail: Gartner predicts AI-powered search tools will push brands to spend more on PR and earned media as companies compete for visibility inside chatbot-generated answers.
Technology.headline: Gartner Predicts AI Search Will Fatten PR Budgets
Microsoft's Xbox controller refresh kills its underused accessory port
Technology.detail: Microsoft's updated Xbox controller removes the underused accessory port.

Technology.headline: 96% of IT pros use AI now: Their top 7 agentic applications and biggest implementation roadblocks
Technology.detail: A recent study reveals that 96% of IT professionals are currently utilizing AI in their work, highlighting the most popular applications and significant challenges in deploying AI solutions.

Science.headline: Ancient asteroid craters may have sparked Earth's oxygen-producing life
Science.detail: Scientists discovered fossil-like stromatolites inside the Hapcheon impact crater in South Korea, suggesting that asteroid strikes may have created warm, mineral-rich lakes where early oxygen-producing life could flourish and that these crater lakes may have functioned as localized "oxygen oases" before oxygen became widespread across the planet.

Science.headline: Ancient chemistry trick unlocks new type of glass that traps CO2 and hydrogen
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered how to fine-tune a futuristic type of porous glass that can trap gases like CO2 and hydrogen, using a technique inspired by centuries-old glassmaking by adding sodium and lithium compounds. This could accelerate the development of high-performance materials for clean energy, gas storage, and advanced manufacturing.

Science.headline: Seagrass found to 'give birth' to new genetic individuals rather than clone itself, offering hope for our underwater gardens
Science.detail: New research shows that seagrass reproduces sexually, delivering new genetically diverse seedlings that disperse on ocean currents to form new meadows. This discovery offers a conservation opportunity, as seedlings could be collected to form new meadows or rejuvenate struggling ones, but it also warns that relying too heavily on seagrass plants from specific patches could leave replanted meadows inbred and vulnerable.

Science.headline: Not all “zombie cells” are bad — and the future of anti-aging may depend on knowing which ones to keep alive
Science.detail: Scientists are uncovering that some aging cells may damage the body, while others help protect it, fueling a new wave of precision anti-aging therapies aimed at removing only the harmful “zombie” cells without disrupting the body's natural repair systems. A new review explores how cellular senescence shapes aging throughout the body and examines the growing push toward precision anti-aging therapies.
Luminescence dating has confirmed Roman exploitation of alluvial gold in the Eastern Pyrenees for the first time. The dating confirms the Romans mined for gold in the area.
Science.headline: Luminescence dating has confirmed Roman exploitation of alluvial gold in the Eastern Pyrenees

Finance & Economy.detail: The UK borrowed more than expected in April, as inflation drove up benefit costs, putting pressure on the government's fiscal plans.
Finance & Economy.headline: UK borrows more than forecast in April as inflation adds to benefits bill
India's forex reserves fall by $8.09 bn to $688.89 bn as of May 15
Finance & Economy.detail: India's foreign exchange reserves decreased by $8.09 billion, reaching $688.89 billion as of May 15, 2026.
US stock futures are slightly up as investors are closely watching the peace talks between the US and Iran.
Finance & Economy.headline: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stock futures edge higher as investors monitor US-Iran peace talks.

Finance & Economy.detail: The yield on the US 10-year Treasury note declined to 4.54% on Friday, marking its lowest level in about a week, due to easing inflation concerns and optimism regarding a potential US-Iran agreement.
Finance & Economy.headline: Treasury Yields Fall for 3rd Session
SpaceX to explore "the true nature of the universe" as it plans IPO
Finance & Economy.detail: Elon Musk's SpaceX is proceeding with its plans to go public, in what is anticipated to be the largest IPO ever, with the goal of exploring "the true nature of the universe".

Sports.detail: Meghan Duggan, a former U.S. Olympic ice hockey captain, is leaving her role with the NHL's New Jersey Devils to become the general manager of the new Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) expansion team in Hamilton.
Sports.headline: US Olympian Meghan Duggan leaves NHL Devils to take over as GM of PWHL expansion team in Hamilton

Sports.detail: Pep Guardiola has confirmed that he will be leaving Manchester City after 10 years at the club, stating that 'nothing is eternal'.
Sports.headline: Guardiola confirms he is leaving Manchester City after 10 years
WNBA warns the Fever for failing to report Caitlin Clark's injury on time
Sports.detail: The WNBA has warned the Indiana Fever for not promptly reporting Caitlin Clark's injury.

Sports.headline: 2-time NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dead at 41
Sports.detail: Kyle Busch, a two-time NASCAR champion, has passed away at the age of 41.
World champion pairs figure skaters, Alisa Efimova-Hase and Ruben Volodin, have announced they will continue competing with the goal of participating in the 2030 Olympics.
Sports.headline: German pair skating stars Hase/Volodin continuing to 2030 Olympics
